name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَن نُّؤْمِنَ بِهَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانِ وَلَا بِٱلَّذِى بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ ۗ وَلَوْ تَرَىٰٓ إِذِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ مَوْقُوفُونَ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ يَرْجِعُ بَعْضُهُمْ إِلَىٰ بَعْضٍ ٱلْقَوْلَ يَقُولُ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتُضْعِفُوا۟ لِلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ لَوْلَآ أَنتُمْ لَكُنَّا مُؤْمِنِينَ (٣١)
```

```english
The faithless say, ‘We will never believe in this Quran, nor in what was [revealed] before it.’ But if you were to see when the wrongdoers will be made to stop before their Lord casting the blame on one another. Those who were abased will say to those who were arrogant, ‘Had it not been for you, we would surely have been faithful.’ (31)
```

```arabic
قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ لِلَّذِينَ ٱسْتُضْعِفُوٓا۟ أَنَحْنُ صَدَدْنَـٰكُمْ عَنِ ٱلْهُدَىٰ بَعْدَ إِذْ جَآءَكُم ۖ بَلْ كُنتُم مُّجْرِمِينَ (٣٢)
```

```english
Those who were arrogant will say to those who were abased, ‘Did we keep you from guidance after it had come to you? No, you were guilty [yourselves].’ (32)
```

```arabic
وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتُضْعِفُوا۟ لِلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ بَلْ مَكْرُ ٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ إِذْ تَأْمُرُونَنَآ أَن نَّكْفُرَ بِٱللَّهِ وَنَجْعَلَ لَهُۥٓ أَندَادًا ۚ وَأَسَرُّوا۟ ٱلنَّدَامَةَ لَمَّا رَأَوُا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ وَجَعَلْنَا ٱلْأَغْلَـٰلَ فِىٓ أَعْنَاقِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ۚ هَلْ يُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا مَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (٣٣)
```

```english
Those who were abased will say to those who were arrogant, ‘No, [it was your] night and day plotting, when you prompted us to forswear Allah and to set up equals to Him.’ They will hide their remorse when they sight the punishment, and We will put iron collars around the necks of the faithless. Shall they not be requited for what they used to do? (33)
```

```arabic
وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَا فِى قَرْيَةٍ مِّن نَّذِيرٍ إِلَّا قَالَ مُتْرَفُوهَآ إِنَّا بِمَآ أُرْسِلْتُم بِهِۦ كَـٰفِرُونَ (٣٤)
```

```english
We did not send a warner to any town without its affluent ones saying, ‘We indeed disbelieve in what you have been sent with.’ (34)
```

```arabic
وَقَالُوا۟ نَحْنُ أَكْثَرُ أَمْوَٰلًا وَأَوْلَـٰدًا وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمُعَذَّبِينَ (٣٥)
```

```english
They say, ‘We have greater wealth and more children, and we will not be punished!’ (35)
```

```arabic
قُلْ إِنَّ رَبِّى يَبْسُطُ ٱلرِّزْقَ لِمَن يَشَآءُ وَيَقْدِرُ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (٣٦)
```

```english
Say, ‘Indeed my Lord expands the provision for whomever He wishes and He tightens it, but most people do not know.’ (36)
```

```arabic
وَمَآ أَمْوَٰلُكُمْ وَلَآ أَوْلَـٰدُكُم بِٱلَّتِى تُقَرِّبُكُمْ عِندَنَا زُلْفَىٰٓ إِلَّا مَنْ ءَامَنَ وَعَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُمْ جَزَآءُ ٱلضِّعْفِ بِمَا عَمِلُوا۟ وَهُمْ فِى ٱلْغُرُفَـٰتِ ءَامِنُونَ (٣٧)
```

```english
It is not your wealth, nor your children, that will bring you close to Us in nearness, excepting those who have faith and act righteously. It is they for whom there will be a twofold reward for what they did, and they will be secure in lofty abodes. (37)
```

```arabic
وَٱلَّذِينَ يَسْعَوْنَ فِىٓ ءَايَـٰتِنَا مُعَـٰجِزِينَ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ فِى ٱلْعَذَابِ مُحْضَرُونَ (٣٨)
```

```english
As for those who contend with Our signs seeking to frustrate [their purpose], they will be brought to the punishment. (38)
```

```arabic
قُلْ إِنَّ رَبِّى يَبْسُطُ ٱلرِّزْقَ لِمَن يَشَآءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِۦ وَيَقْدِرُ لَهُۥ ۚ وَمَآ أَنفَقْتُم مِّن شَىْءٍ فَهُوَ يُخْلِفُهُۥ ۖ وَهُوَ خَيْرُ ٱلرَّٰزِقِينَ (٣٩)
```

```english
Say, ‘Indeed my Lord expands the provision for whomever of His servants that He wishes and tightens it, and He will repay whatever you may spend, and He is the best of providers.’ (39)
```

```arabic
وَيَوْمَ يَحْشُرُهُمْ جَمِيعًا ثُمَّ يَقُولُ لِلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ أَهَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ إِيَّاكُمْ كَانُوا۟ يَعْبُدُونَ (٤٠)
```

```english
On the day He will muster them all together, He will say to the angels, ‘Was it you that these used to worship?’ (40)
```

```arabic
قَالُوا۟ سُبْحَـٰنَكَ أَنتَ وَلِيُّنَا مِن دُونِهِم ۖ بَلْ كَانُوا۟ يَعْبُدُونَ ٱلْجِنَّ ۖ أَكْثَرُهُم بِهِم مُّؤْمِنُونَ (٤١)
```

```english
They will say, ‘Immaculate are You! You are our wali, not they! No, they used to worship the jinn; most of them had faith in them.’ (41)
```

```arabic
فَٱلْيَوْمَ لَا يَمْلِكُ بَعْضُكُمْ لِبَعْضٍ نَّفْعًا وَلَا ضَرًّا وَنَقُولُ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ذُوقُوا۟ عَذَابَ ٱلنَّارِ ٱلَّتِى كُنتُم بِهَا تُكَذِّبُونَ (٤٢)
```

```english
‘Today you have no power to benefit or harm one another,’ and We shall say to those who did wrong, ‘Taste the punishment of the Fire which you used to deny.’ (42)
```

```arabic
وَإِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ ءَايَـٰتُنَا بَيِّنَـٰتٍ قَالُوا۟ مَا هَـٰذَآ إِلَّا رَجُلٌ يُرِيدُ أَن يَصُدَّكُمْ عَمَّا كَانَ يَعْبُدُ ءَابَآؤُكُمْ وَقَالُوا۟ مَا هَـٰذَآ إِلَّآ إِفْكٌ مُّفْتَرًى ۚ وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لِلْحَقِّ لَمَّا جَآءَهُمْ إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ (٤٣)
```

```english
When Our manifest signs are recited to them, they say, ‘This is just a man who desires to keep you from what your fathers used to worship.’ And they say, ‘This is nothing but a fabricated lie.’ The faithless say of the truth when it comes to them: ‘This is nothing but plain magic,’ (43)
```

```arabic
وَمَآ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُم مِّن كُتُبٍ يَدْرُسُونَهَا ۖ وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَآ إِلَيْهِمْ قَبْلَكَ مِن نَّذِيرٍ (٤٤)
```

```english
though We did not give them any scriptures that they might have studied, nor did We send them any warner before you. (44)
```

```arabic
وَكَذَّبَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ وَمَا بَلَغُوا۟ مِعْشَارَ مَآ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمْ فَكَذَّبُوا۟ رُسُلِى ۖ فَكَيْفَ كَانَ نَكِيرِ (٤٥)
```

```english
Those who were before them had also denied—and these have not attained one-tenth of what We had given them—they impugned My apostles, so how was My rebuttal! (45)
```

```arabic
۞ قُلْ إِنَّمَآ أَعِظُكُم بِوَٰحِدَةٍ ۖ أَن تَقُومُوا۟ لِلَّهِ مَثْنَىٰ وَفُرَٰدَىٰ ثُمَّ تَتَفَكَّرُوا۟ ۚ مَا بِصَاحِبِكُم مِّن جِنَّةٍ ۚ إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ لَّكُم بَيْنَ يَدَىْ عَذَابٍ شَدِيدٍ (٤٦)
```

```english
Say, ‘I give you just a single advice: that you rise up for Allah’s sake, in pairs or singly, and then reflect: there is no madness in your companion; he is just a warner to you before [the befalling of] a severe punishment.’ (46)
```

```arabic
قُلْ مَا سَأَلْتُكُم مِّنْ أَجْرٍ فَهُوَ لَكُمْ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ شَهِيدٌ (٤٧)
```

```english
Say, ‘Whatever reward I may have asked you is for your own good. My [true] reward lies only with Allah, and He is witness to all things.’ (47)
```

```arabic
قُلْ إِنَّ رَبِّى يَقْذِفُ بِٱلْحَقِّ عَلَّـٰمُ ٱلْغُيُوبِ (٤٨)
```

```english
Say, ‘Indeed my Lord hurls the truth. [He is] the knower of all that is Unseen.’ (48)
```

```arabic
قُلْ جَآءَ ٱلْحَقُّ وَمَا يُبْدِئُ ٱلْبَـٰطِلُ وَمَا يُعِيدُ (٤٩)
```

```english
Say, ‘The truth has come, and falsehood neither originates nor restores [anything].’ (49)
```

```arabic
قُلْ إِن ضَلَلْتُ فَإِنَّمَآ أَضِلُّ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِى ۖ وَإِنِ ٱهْتَدَيْتُ فَبِمَا يُوحِىٓ إِلَىَّ رَبِّىٓ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ سَمِيعٌ قَرِيبٌ (٥٠)
```

```english
Say, ‘If I go astray, my going astray is only to my own harm, and if I am rightly guided that is because of what my Lord has revealed to me. Indeed He is all-hearing and nearmost.’ (50)
```

```arabic
وَلَوْ تَرَىٰٓ إِذْ فَزِعُوا۟ فَلَا فَوْتَ وَأُخِذُوا۟ مِن مَّكَانٍ قَرِيبٍ (٥١)
```

```english
Were you to see them when they will be stricken with terror, without any escape, and are seized from a close quarter. (51)
```

```arabic
وَقَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا بِهِۦ وَأَنَّىٰ لَهُمُ ٱلتَّنَاوُشُ مِن مَّكَانٍۭ بَعِيدٍ (٥٢)
```

```english
They will say, ‘We believe in it [now]!’ But how can they attain it from a far-off place, (52)
```

```arabic
وَقَدْ كَفَرُوا۟ بِهِۦ مِن قَبْلُ ۖ وَيَقْذِفُونَ بِٱلْغَيْبِ مِن مَّكَانٍۭ بَعِيدٍ (٥٣)
```

```english
when they denied it in the past—shooting at something invisible from a far-off place— (53)
```

```arabic
وَحِيلَ بَيْنَهُمْ وَبَيْنَ مَا يَشْتَهُونَ كَمَا فُعِلَ بِأَشْيَاعِهِم مِّن قَبْلُ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ فِى شَكٍّ مُّرِيبٍۭ (٥٤)
```

```english
and a barrier is set between them and what they desire, as was done aforetime with their likes, who had remained in grave doubt? (54)
```

## (Commentary)

This is another section of the verses of the Surah that discusses the matter of prophethood (النبوة) and what pertains to it, as well as what the polytheists say regarding it. In its course, it concludes with what will befall them on the day of death or the Day of Resurrection. It is connected to His saying in the previous section: **وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَاكَ إِلَّا كَافَّةً لِلنَّاسِ** — "We did not send you except as a [bearer of good news and warner] to all mankind" (34:28). As you have seen, that verse acts like an intermediate realm (البرزخ) — *the state between two stages* — between the two sections, mentioning the messengership (الرسالة) and making it a proof for monotheism (التوحيد) — *the absolute oneness of Allah in His essence, attributes, and acts*.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَنْ نُؤْمِنَ بِهَذَا الْقُرْآنِ وَلَا بِالَّذِي بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ** — "The faithless say, ‘We will never believe in this Quran, nor in what was before it’" (34:31). What is meant by "those who disbelieve" (الذين كفروا) are the polytheists, and what is meant by "what was before it" (الذي بين يديه) are the Heavenly Books such as the Torah and the Gospel. This is because the polytheists, being idolaters (الوثنيون), do not believe in prophethood or the Heavenly Books that follow it.

The claim of some that "what was before it" refers to the matter of the Hereafter is not supported by any evidence. The Quran frequently uses the expression "what was before it" to refer to the Torah and the Gospel. Similarly, it is an error for some to claim that "those who disbelieve" refers to the Jews.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **وَلَوْ تَرَى إِذِ الظَّالِمُونَ مَوْقُوفُونَ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ** — "Were you to see when the wrongdoers are made to stand before their Lord..." (34:31). It appears that the definite article (اللام) in "the wrongdoers" (الظالمون) is for specific reference (للعهد). This verse and the two following it indicate that the evil consequence of this disbelief—the foundation of which is the deviation of the leaders of disbelief and their leading their followers astray—will overtake them, and they will regret it, but regret shall not avail them.

Thus, His saying: **وَلَوْ تَرَى** — "Were you to see" is an address to the Prophet صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله, as they are far removed from understanding the address. **إِذِ الظَّالِمُونَ** — "when the wrongdoers"—and they are those who disbelieved in the Books of Allah and His Messengers, who wronged themselves through disbelief—**مَوْقُوفُونَ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ** — "are made to stand before their Lord" for reckoning and recompense on the Day of Resurrection. **يَرْجِعُ بَعْضُهُمْ إِلَى بَعْضٍ الْقَوْلَ** — "casting the blame on one another"—meaning they converse and exchange words in dispute. **يَقُولُ الَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا** — "Those who were abased will say"—this is an exposition of their casting blame on one another. The "abased" (المستضعفون) are the followers whom the leaders had weakened. **لِلَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا** — "to those who were arrogant"—and they are the leaders and guides. **لَوْلَا أَنْتُمْ لَكُنَّا مُؤْمِنِينَ** — "Had it not been for you, we would surely have been believers"—meaning you forced us into disbelief and stood between us and faith.

**قَالَ الَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا لِلَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا** — "Those who were arrogant will say to those who were abased" in response to their statement and in rejection of the accusation of compulsion and coercion: **أَنَحْنُ صَدَدْنَاكُمْ** — "Was it we who barred you"—the interrogation here is for denial (للإنكار), meaning: did we turn you away—**عَنِ الْهُدَى بَعْدَ إِذْ جَاءَكُمْ** — "from the guidance after it had come to you?" Its reaching you through the prophetic call is the strongest proof that we did not stand between it and you; rather, you were free to choose between believing in it or disbelieving. **بَلْ كُنْتُمْ مُجْرِمِينَ** — "No, you were [yourselves] guilty"—clothed in criminality and persisting in it. You committed the crime of disbelief when it came to you without us forcing you; thus, your disbelief originated from yourselves, and we are innocent of it.

**وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا لِلَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا** — "Those who were abased will say to those who were arrogant" in response to their words and their claim of innocence: **بَلْ مَكْرُ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ** — "Rather, it was a stratagem of night and day"—meaning your plotting by night and day drove us to disbelief—**إِذْ تَأْمُرُونَنَا أَنْ نَكْفُرَ بِاللَّهِ وَنَجْعَلَ لَهُ أَنْدَادًا** — "when you would bid us to disbelieve in Allah and to set up equals to Him"—and peers from among the deities. That is, you never ceased in the worldly life to plot by night and day and to devise plans to weaken us and conspire against us, forcing us to obey you in what you desired. We did not realize it until we were compelled to follow your command when you ordered us toward disbelief and polytheism (الشرك) — *the act of associating partners with Allah*.

**وَأَسَرُّوا** — "And they will hide"—and conceal—**النَّدَامَةَ لَمَّا رَأَوُا الْعَذَابَ** — "their remorse when they see the punishment" and witness that there is no escape. Their concealing of remorse on the Day of Resurrection—which is a day when they are exposed and nothing of them is hidden from Allah—is similar to their lying to Allah, their denial of polytheism, and their swearing to Allah. All of this is part of the manifestation of their vile dispositions (ملكاتهم الرذيلة) that became rooted in their souls. They used to hide remorse in the world for fear of the enemies' gloating, and they do the same on the Day of Resurrection despite the manifestation of what they concealed. That day is the day when "the secrets are examined" (تبلى السرائر), just as they lie due to the disposition of lying despite it being manifest that they are liars in their speech.

Then the Glorified mentioned their seizure for punishment, saying: **وَجَعَلْنَا الْأَغْلَالَ** — "And We will put iron collars"—chains—**فِي أَعْنَاقِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا هَلْ يُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا مَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ** — "around the necks of the faithless. Shall they be requited [with anything] except what they used to do?" (34:33). Thus, their deeds became collars around their necks, imprisoning them in the punishment.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَا فِي قَرْيَةٍ مِنْ نَذِيرٍ إِلَّا قَالَ مُتْرَفُوهَا إِنَّا بِمَا أُرْسِلْتُمْ بِهِ كَافِرُونَ** — "We did not send a warner to any town but its affluent ones said, ‘We indeed disbelieve in what you have been sent with’" (34:34). The "affluent ones" (المترفون) is a passive participle from *itraf* (الإتراف), which is an excess in luxury. This contains an intimation that affluence leads to arrogance (الاستكبار) against the truth, as the subsequent verse indicates.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **وَقَالُوا نَحْنُ أَكْثَرُ أَمْوَالًا وَأَوْلَادًا وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمُعَذَّبِينَ** — "And they said, ‘We are more [than you] in wealth and children, and we will not be punished’" (34:35). The plural pronoun refers to the affluent ones. It is the nature of affluence, luxury, and fluctuating in the blessings of the world that a person's heart becomes attached to them and aggrandizes them. Thus, he sees happiness (السعادة) within them, whether it accords with the truth or opposes it. He remembers only the outward aspect of life and forgets what lies beyond it.

Therefore, the Glorified recounted this from them when they said: **نَحْنُ أَكْثَرُ أَمْوَالًا وَأَوْلَادًا** — "We are more in wealth and children"—implying there is no happiness except in them and no wretchedness with them—**وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمُعَذَّبِينَ** — "and we will not be punished" in the Hereafter. They did not deny the punishment except out of heedlessness and turning away from what lies beyond the abundance of wealth and children. Since these alone constituted happiness and success, punishment would only lie in their loss, and no punishment could exist alongside them.

There is another perspective: due to their delusion regarding the wealth and children they were provided, they thought they possessed a station of nobility (كرامة) before Allah the Glorified, and that they would remain in their nobility as long as they existed. The meaning would be: we are possessors of nobility before Allah by what we have been given of abundant wealth and children, and we shall remain in our nobility; thus, we are not to be punished even if there were a punishment.

In this sense, the verse is similar to His saying: **وَلَئِنْ أَذَقْنَاهُ رَحْمَةً مِنَّا مِنْ بَعْدِ ضَرَّاءَ مَسَّتْهُ لَيَقُولَنَّ هَذَا لِي وَمَا أَظُنُّ السَّاعَةَ قَائِمَةً وَلَئِنْ رُجِعْتُ إِلَى رَبِّي إِنَّ لِي عِنْدَهُ لَلْحُسْنَى** — "And if We let him taste a mercy from Us after some adversity has touched him, he will surely say, ‘This is my due! I do not think the Hour will ever set in. And even if I am returned to my Lord, I will indeed have the best [reward] with Him’" (41:50).

**His saying, the Exalted**: **قُلْ إِنَّ رَبِّي يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَنْ يَشَاءُ وَيَقْدِرُ وَلَكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ** — "Say, ‘Indeed my Lord expands the provision for whomever He wishes and He tightens it, but most people do not know’" (34:36). This verse and the three following it until the completion of four verses are a response to their saying: "We are more in wealth..." etc. This has been answered in two ways. First, the matter of provision (الرزق) in terms of wealth and children—its expansion and restriction—is in the hand of Allah according to what wisdom (الحكمة) and interest (المصلحة) demand and the causes He has prepared, not by the will of man nor due to his nobility before Allah. He may expand the provision for a believer or a disbeliever, for a wise person of resolve or a light-headed fool. He may expand it for one and then restrict it. Thus, affluence is no indication of happiness or nobility.

This is the meaning of His saying: **قُلْ إِنَّ رَبِّي** — "Say, ‘Indeed my Lord’"—He attributed it to Himself because they did not view Allah as their Lord, and provision is among the affairs of Lordship (الربوبية). **يَبْسُطُ** — "expands"—meaning He widens—**الرِّزْقَ لِمَنْ يَشَاءُ** — "the provision for whomever He wishes" of His servants according to wisdom and interest. **وَيَقْدِرُ** — "and He tightens it"—meaning He restricts it. **وَلَكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ** — "but most people do not know"—so they attribute what they are not given to apparent accidental causes, and when they are given, they attribute it to their own resolve and good management. This is sufficient proof of foolishness.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **وَمَا أَمْوَالُكُمْ وَلَا أَوْلَادُكُمْ بِالَّتِي تُقَرِّبُكُمْ عِنْدَنَا زُلْفَى** — "It is not your wealth or your children that will bring you near to Us in station..." (34:37). This is the second response to their saying: "We are more in wealth and children, and we will not be punished." Its summary is that the negation of punishment—which results from proximity (القرب) to Allah—does not result from wealth and children. Wealth and children do not necessitate proximity or a station (زلفى) before Allah such that divine punishment would be negated. Thus, placing the "bringing near" of wealth in the verse in the place of the "negation of punishment" is like placing the cause in the place of the effect.

This is the meaning of His saying: **وَمَا أَمْوَالُكُمْ وَلَا أَوْلَادُكُمْ** — "It is not your wealth or your children" upon which you rely for happiness and the negation of Allah's punishment—**بِالَّتِي** — "that [group which]"—**تُقَرِّبُكُمْ عِنْدَنَا زُلْفَى** — "will bring you near to Us in station"—meaning in proximity.

**إِلَّا مَنْ آمَنَ وَعَمِلَ صَالِحًا** — "except those who have faith and act righteously"—regarding their wealth and children, by spending their wealth in the way of Allah and disseminating faith and righteous deeds (العمل الصالح) among their children through religious upbringing. **فَأُولَئِكَ لَهُمْ جَزَاءُ الضِّعْفِ** — "for such there will be a twofold reward"—perhaps this is the addition of the described to the attribute, meaning "the doubled reward" (الجزاء المضاعف), because they were guided and they guided others, and also because of the doubling of good deeds to ten times their like and more. **وَهُمْ فِي الْغُرُفَاتِ** — "and they will be in the high chambers"—meaning in lofty pavilions—**آمِنُونَ** — "secure" from punishment; thus, they are not to be punished.

**وَالَّذِينَ يَسْعَوْنَ فِي آيَاتِنَا مُعَاجِزِينَ** — "As for those who strive to frustrate Our signs"—meaning they exert themselves against Our signs intending to outmaneuver Us or to outrun Us—**أُولَئِكَ فِي الْعَذَابِ مُحْضَرُونَ** — "they shall be hauled into the punishment" even if their wealth and children are abundant.

In His saying: **وَمَا أَمْوَالُكُمْ وَلَا أَوْلَادُكُمْ** etc., there is a transition to addressing people in general, disbelievers and others. The reason for this is that the ruling He mentioned regarding wealth and children is the same for both the believer and the disbeliever. Wealth and children only produce their beautiful effect if there is faith and righteous deeds associated with them; otherwise, they only increase one in evil consequence (وبال).

**His saying, the Exalted**: **قُلْ إِنَّ رَبِّي يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَنْ يَشَاءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ وَيَقْدِرُ لَهُ وَمَا أَنْفَقْتُمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ فَهُوَ يُخْلِفُهُ وَهُوَ خَيْرُ الرَّازِقِينَ** — "Say, ‘Indeed my Lord expands the provision for whomever He wishes of His servants and tightens it for him, and He will replace whatever thing you spend, and He is the best of providers’" (34:39). It is said in *al-Majma' al-Bayan*: "It is said: 'Allah replaced (أخلف) for him and upon him' when He substitutes for him what has left him." End quote.

The context of the verse indicates that what is meant by "spending" (الإنفاق) here is spending in ways of goodness (وجوه البر). The intent is to explain that this type of spending is not lost with Allah; rather, He replaces it and provides a substitute for it.

Thus, His saying at the beginning of the verse: **قُلْ إِنَّ رَبِّي يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَنْ يَشَاءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ وَيَقْدِرُ** — "Say, ‘Indeed my Lord expands the provision for whomever He wishes of His servants and tightens it’" is to indicate that the matter of provision in its expansion and restriction is up to Allah the Glorified; it does not decrease by spending nor increase by withholding. Then He said: **وَمَا أَنْفَقْتُمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ** — "and He will replace whatever thing you spend"—whether little or much, and whatever kind of wealth it may be—**فَهُوَ يُخْلِفُهُ** — "He will replace it" and provide you its substitute either in this world or in the Hereafter. **وَهُوَ خَيْرُ الرَّازِقِينَ** — "and He is the best of providers"—for He provides out of generosity (جودا), whereas the provision of others is, in reality, a transaction and an exchange. Furthermore, He is the Provider in reality, and others who are called providers are merely intermediaries for the provision to reach its destination.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **وَيَوْمَ يَحْشُرُهُمْ جَمِيعًا ثُمَّ يَقُولُ لِلْمَلَائِكَةِ أَهَؤُلَاءِ إِيَّاكُمْ كَانُوا يَعْبُدُونَ** — "On the day He will muster them all together, then He will say to the angels, ‘Was it you that these used to worship?’" (34:40). What is meant by "all together" (جميعا), as evidenced by the context, are the worshippers and the worshipped together.

His saying: **ثُمَّ يَقُولُ لِلْمَلَائِكَةِ أَهَؤُلَاءِ إِيَّاكُمْ كَانُوا يَعْبُدُونَ** — "then He will say to the angels, ‘Was it you that these used to worship?’" is not a question seeking information about the fact of their worship. If it were so, they would not be able to deny it, for they did worship them in the world, yet they denied it as in the verse. Rather, the intent is to ask about their *approval* of being worshipped, similar to His saying to Jesus son of Mary: **أَأَنْتَ قُلْتَ لِلنَّاسِ اتَّخِذُونِي وَأُمِّيَ إِلَهَيْنِ مِنْ دُونِ اللَّهِ** — "Did you say to the people, ‘Take me and my mother for two gods besides Allah’?" (5:116).

The purpose of the question is to rebuke the polytheists and make them despair of the help of the angels and their intercession (الشفاعة) — *the act of mediating on behalf of others before Allah* — for them, as they had worshipped them in the world for that purpose.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **قَالُوا سُبْحَانَكَ أَنْتَ وَلِيُّنَا مِنْ دُونِهِمْ بَلْ كَانُوا يَعْبُدُونَ الْجِنَّ أَكْثَرُهُمْ بِهِمْ مُؤْمِنُونَ** — "They will say, ‘Immaculate are You! You are our guardian, not they! Rather they used to worship the jinn; most of them believed in them’" (34:41). The angels, in their response to His question, adopted the highest forms of etiquette. First, they glorified Him with an absolute glorification (تنزيه) that includes His being far above having any worshipped besides Him. Then they denied their approval of the polytheists' worship of them, but not by explicitly denying approval nor by even uttering the word "worship" in relation to themselves, so as to protect the presence of the Addressed from what might strike the ear with such a notion, even if conceived and not affirmed. Rather, they answered by restricting their guardianship (الولاية) to Him and denying it from the polytheists, to indicate the denial of approval of their worship through metonymy (الكناية). For approval of their worship necessitates mutual friendship (الموالاة) between them, and mutual friendship between them contradicts the restriction of guardianship to Allah the Glorified. If guardianship is confined to Him, there is no mutual friendship, and if there is no mutual friendship, there is no approval.

Then they said, as recounted by Allah the Glorified: **بَلْ كَانُوا يَعْبُدُونَ الْجِنَّ أَكْثَرُهُمْ بِهِمْ مُؤْمِنُونَ** — "Rather they used to worship the jinn; most of them believed in them." The jinn are the second of the three groups worshipped by the idolaters, which are: the angels, the jinn, and the saintly among humans. The most ancient in deserving worship according to them are the first two groups, while the third group is joined to them after reaching perfection, even if they are superior to them.

The transition (الإضراب) in their saying: **بَلْ كَانُوا يَعْبُدُونَ الْجِنَّ** — "Rather they used to worship the jinn" indicates that the jinn were pleased with their worship of them.

These among the jinn are those whom the idolaters consider the principles of evil in the world; thus, they worship them to seek protection from their evils, just as they worship the angels out of desire for their blessings, as they are the principles of good. This is not as some have said: that the jinn refers to Iblis, his progeny, and his tribe, and that the meaning of worshipping them is obeying them in what they called them to, such as worshipping the angels or sins in general. This is refuted by the use of the word "faith" (الإيمان) in the verse rather than "obedience" (الطاعة). Nor is it as some said: that they used to manifest to them and make them imagine they were angels so they would worship them. Nor as some said: that they used to enter the hollows of idols when worshipped, thus being worshipped through the worship of the idols.

Perhaps the reason for attributing faith in them to "most of them" (أكثرهم) rather than all of them is that most of them worship the deities to seek protection from the occurrence of evil from them, and the principles of evil according to them are absolutely the jinn. This is not as some said: that "most" means "all," which is based on interpreting worship as obedience, the flaw of which you have already seen.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **فَالْيَوْمَ لَا يَمْلِكُ بَعْضُكُمْ لِبَعْضٍ نَفْعًا وَلَا ضَرًّا وَنَقُولُ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا ذُوقُوا عَذَابَ النَّارِ الَّتِي كُنْتُمْ بِهَا تُكَذِّبُونَ** — "‘Today you will have no power to benefit or harm one another.’ And We shall say to the wrongdoers, ‘Taste the punishment of the Fire which you used to deny!’" (34:42). This is a type of derivation from the angels' disavowal of them. The disavowal of all the followed from their followers and the followers from their followed has been explained in various places, such as His saying: **وَيَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ يَكْفُرُونَ بِشِرْكِكُمْ** — "and on the Day of Resurrection they will disown your polytheism" (35:14) and His saying: **ثُمَّ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ يَكْفُرُ بَعْضُكُمْ بِبَعْضٍ وَيَلْعَنُ بَعْضُكُمْ بَعْضًا** — "Then on the Day of Resurrection you will disown one another and curse one another" (29:25). The meaning of the verse is clear.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **وَإِذَا تُتْلَى عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتُنَا بَيِّنَاتٍ قَالُوا مَا هَذَا إِلَّا رَجُلٌ يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَصُدَّكُمْ عَمَّا كَانَ يَعْبُدُ آبَاؤُكُمْ** — "When Our manifest signs are recited to them, they say, ‘This is just a man who wants to bar you from what your fathers used to worship’" (34:43). This address of theirs to their common folk after hearing the verses is to alert them to be serious in clinging to the religion of their fathers and to incite them against him صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله. In describing the verses as "manifest" (بينات), there is a type of reproach, as if it were said: when these verses are recited to them, and they are manifest with no doubt in them, instead of calling their common folk to follow them, they urged them to persist in imitating their fathers and incited them to it. In adding "fathers" to the pronoun in "bar you" (يصدكم), there is an exaggeration in incitement and provocation.

And His saying: **وَقَالُوا مَا هَذَا إِلَّا إِفْكٌ مُفْتَرًى** — "And they say, ‘This is nothing but a fabricated lie’" is conjoined to "they say," meaning: and they said, referring to the manifest signs with a gesture of contempt: this is nothing but speech turned away from its proper course, lied about against Allah, instead of saying: they are manifest signs sent down from Allah the Exalted. They referred to the manifest signs with "this" (هذا) to indicate that they understood nothing from them except that they were "something" and nothing more.

Then the Glorified changed the context and said: **وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لِلْحَقِّ لَمَّا جَاءَهُمْ إِنْ هَذَا إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُبِينٌ** — "And the faithless say of the truth when it comes to them, ‘This is nothing but plain magic’" (34:43). The "coming of the truth" to them is its reaching them and its manifestation to them. Adopting the description of "disbelief" (الكفر) is to intimate the cause (للإشعار بالتعليل). The meaning is: those who disbelieved were driven by their disbelief to say of the explicit truth that reached them and was manifest to them: this is magic whose magical nature and falsehood are evident.

He emphasized their persistence in refuting the truth by following whims without any evidence by His saying: **وَمَا آتَيْنَاهُمْ مِنْ كُتُبٍ يَدْرُسُونَهَا وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَا إِلَيْهِمْ قَبْلَكَ مِنْ نَذِيرٍ** — "We have not given them any books that they might study, nor did We send them any warner before you" (34:44). The sentence is a circumstantial clause (حالية), meaning: those who disbelieved—the disbelievers of the Quraysh—called the explicit truth manifest to them "plain magic," while the case is that We did not give them books to study so they might distinguish truth from falsehood, nor did We send them a messenger before you to warn them and clarify that to them, such that they could say, based on a Divine Book or the word of a warning Messenger, that it is truth or falsehood.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **وَكَذَّبَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ وَمَا بَلَغُوا مِعْشَارَ مَا آتَيْنَاهُمْ فَكَذَّبُوا رُسُلِي فَكَيْفَ كَانَ نَكِيرِ** — "Those who were before them [also] denied, and these have not attained a tenth of what We had given them. But they denied My apostles; so how was My rebuke!" (34:45). The first and second plural pronouns refer to the disbelievers of the Quraysh and those who follow them, while the third and fourth refer to those before them. The **mishar** (المعشار) is one-tenth, and **nakir** (النكير) is denial, and what is meant by it in the verse is its consequence, which is the seizure by punishment.

The meaning is: those who were before the disbelievers of the Quraysh among the past nations denied the truth of the signs, and the disbelievers of the Quraysh have not reached one-tenth of the strength and intensity We gave them. Yet those peoples denied My messengers; so how was My seizure by punishment? And how insignificant is the matter of the Quraysh. The shift (الالتفات) in the verse to the first person is to aggrandize the crime and to make the reckoning more terrifying.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **قُلْ إِنَّمَا أَعِظُكُمْ بِوَاحِدَةٍ أَنْ تَقُومُوا لِلَّهِ مَثْنَى وَفُرَادَى ثُمَّ تَتَفَكَّرُوا مَا بِصَاحِبِكُمْ مِنْ جِنَّةٍ** — "Say, ‘I give you just one admonition: that you arise for Allah’s sake, in pairs and singly, and then reflect: there is no madness in your companion’" (34:46). What is meant by "admonition" (الموعظة) is "counsel" (الوصية) by way of metonymy or implication. His saying: **أَنْ تَقُومُوا لِلَّهِ** — "that you arise for Allah's sake" means you stand up for the sake of Allah and His noble countenance. His saying: **مَثْنَى وَفُرَادَى** — "in pairs and singly"—meaning two by two and one by one—is a metonymy for dispersing and avoiding the gathering and the rabble, for the rabble possesses neither consciousness nor thought, and often kills the truth and revives falsehood.

His saying: **مَا بِصَاحِبِكُمْ مِنْ جِنَّةٍ** — "there is no madness in your companion" is a new sentence (استئناف). **إِنَّمَا** is negatory, and this is evidenced by His saying afterward: **إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ لَكُمْ بَيْنَ يَدَيْ عَذَابٍ شَدِيدٍ** — "He is just a warner to you before a severe punishment" (34:46). It is also possible that **إِنَّمَا** is interrogative or a relative pronoun, and **مِنْ جِنَّةٍ** is its exposition.

What is meant by "your companion" (صاحبكم) is the Prophet صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله himself. The reason for using this expression is to remind them of his long companionship with them for forty years, from the time of his birth until his mission, so they might remember that they never knew from him any disorder in thought, lightness of opinion, or anything that would suggest he possessed madness.

The meaning is: Say to them: I only counsel you with one admonition: that you arise and stand for the sake of Allah, dispersed so that your thought may become clear and your opinion sound, two by two and one by one, and reflect upon my matter. I have accompanied you throughout my life with soundness of opinion, truthfulness, and trustworthiness; there is no madness in me. I am nothing but a warner to you before a severe punishment on the Day of Resurrection; thus, I am a sincere counselor to you, not a traitor.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **قُلْ مَا سَأَلْتُكُمْ مِنْ أَجْرٍ فَهُوَ لَكُمْ** — "Say, ‘Whatever reward I may have asked of you, it is for yourselves’" (34:47). This is a metonymy for not asking for any reward for the call. For if he grants them whatever reward he asked of them, then he has no reward to ask of them, and the consequence is that he does not ask them. This is to soothe their souls so they do not accuse him of making the call a means to attain wealth or status.

Then he completed the statement by saying: **إِنْ أَجْرِيَ إِلَّا عَلَى اللَّهِ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ شَهِيدٌ** — "My reward lies only with Allah, and He is witness to all things." This is so that his statement is not rejected as an unheard claim, for a person does not intend an action without a goal. He refuted this by saying: my action has a reward, but it is upon Allah, not upon you, and He witnesses my action, and He is witness to all things.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **قُلْ إِنَّ رَبِّي يَقْذِفُ بِالْحقِّ عَلَّامُ الْغُيُوبِ** — "Say, ‘Indeed my Lord hurls the truth [at falsehood], [He is] the Knower of the Unseen’" (34:48). *Qadhf* (القذف) is hurling. His saying: **عَلَّامُ الْغُيُوبِ** — "the Knower of the Unseen" is a predicate after a predicate, or a predicate for a hidden subject, which is the pronoun referring to Him, the Exalted.

The context of the previous verses necessitates that what is meant by the "hurled truth" is the Quran sent down to him by revelation (الوحي) from Him, the Exalted, which is a decisive word that establishes the truth and nullifies falsehood. Thus, it is the truth hurled to him صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله from the Knower of the Unseen, so it smashes falsehood and makes it vanish. He, the Exalted, said: **بَلْ نَقْذِفُ بِالْحَقِّ عَلَى الْبَاطِلِ فَيَدْمَغُهُ فَإِذَا هُوَ زَاهِقٌ** — "Rather We hurl the truth against falsehood, and it smashes its head, and behold, it vanishes" (21:18), and He said: **قُلْ جَاءَ الْحَقُّ وَزَهَقَ الْبَاطِلُ إِنَّ الْبَاطِلَ كَانَ زَهُوقًا** — "Say, ‘The truth has come, and falsehood has vanished. Indeed falsehood is bound to vanish’" (17:81).

**His saying, the Exalted**: **قُلْ جَاءَ الْحَقُّ وَمَا يُبْدِئُ الْبَاطِلُ وَمَا يُعِيدُ** — "Say, ‘The truth has come, and falsehood neither originates [anything] nor restores [it]’" (34:49). What is meant by the "coming of the truth," as guided by the previous verse, is the descent of the Quran which nullifies every falsehood from its root with its definitive proofs and radiant demonstrations.

His saying: **وَمَا يُبْدِئُ الْبَاطِلُ وَمَا يُعِيدُ** — "and falsehood neither originates [anything] nor restores [it]"—meaning it does not manifest a new initial matter after the coming of the truth, nor does it restore a matter it had previously manifested a second time by way of restoration. This is a metonymy for the nullification of falsehood and its falling away from any effect from its root by the truth, which is the Quran.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **قُلْ إِنْ ضَلَلْتُ فَإِنَّمَا أَضِلُّ عَلَى نَفْسِي وَإِنِ اهْتَدَيْتُ فَبِمَا يُوحِي إِلَيَّ رَبِّي إِنَّهُ سَمِيعٌ قَرِيبُ** — "Say, ‘If I go astray, I go astray only to my own loss, and if I am rightly guided, it is because of what my Lord reveals to me. Indeed He is all-hearing, near’" (34:50). This is an exposition of the effect of the truth, which is revelation. For He has defined it as absolute truth. If the truth is truth from every aspect, it does not fail to hit the reality in any aspect; otherwise, it would be false from that aspect. Thus, revelation guides and never errs.

Therefore, He said, emphasizing what preceded: **قُلْ إِنْ ضَلَلْتُ** — "Say, ‘If I go astray’"—supposing that straying occurred from me—**فَإِنَّمَا أَضِلُّ** — "I go astray only"—with that straying resting—**عَلَى نَفْسِي** — "to my own loss," for it is within man's nature to go astray. **وَإِنِ اهْتَدَيْتُ فَبِمَا يُوحِي إِلَيَّ رَبِّي** — "and if I am rightly guided, it is because of what my Lord reveals to me"—for His revelation is truth that admits no straying and produces nothing but guidance.

He reasoned the statement by His saying: **إِنَّهُ سَمِيعٌ قَرِيبٌ** — "Indeed He is all-hearing, near"—to indicate that He hears the call and no barrier of distance veils Him from it. He had previously prepared for this by describing Him, the Exalted, in the hurling of the truth as the "Knower of the Unseen" (علام الغيوب); thus, no matter is hidden from Him that could disrupt His command or prevent the execution of His will in guiding people through revelation. He, the Exalted, said: **عَالِمُ الْغَيْبِ فَلَا يُظْهِرُ عَلَى غَيْبِهِ أَحَدًا إِلَّا مَنِ ارْتَضَى مِنْ رَسُولٍ فَإِنَّهُ يَسْلُكُ مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِ رَصَدًا لِيَعْلَمَ أَنْ قَدْ أَبْلَغُوا رِسَالَاتِ رَبِّهِمْ وَأَحَاطَ بِمَا لَدَيْهِمْ وَأَحْصَى كُلَّ شَيْءٍ عَدَدًا** — "Knower of the Unseen, He does not disclose His Unseen to anyone, except to an apostle He approves of. Then He dispatches a sentinel before him and another behind him, so that He may know that they have delivered the messages of their Lord, and He encompasses all that is with them, and He keeps count of all things" (72:26-28).

**His saying, the Exalted**: **وَلَوْ تَرَى إِذْ فَزِعُوا فَلَا فَوْتَ وَأُخِذُوا مِنْ مَكَانٍ قَرِيبٍ** — "Were you to see when they are struck with terror, and there is no escape, and they are seized from a place nearby" (34:51). The apparent previous context, intimated by His subsequent saying: **وَحِيلَ بَيْنَهُمْ وَبَيْنَ مَا يَشْتَهُونَ كَمَا فُعِلَ بِأَشْيَاعِهِمْ مِنْ قَبْلُ** — "A barrier will be set between them and what they desire, as was done with their likes before" (34:54), is that these four verses describe the state of the polytheists of the Quraysh and those who join them at the time of death.

Thus, His saying: **وَلَوْ تَرَى إِذْ فَزِعُوا** — "Were you to see when they are struck with terror"—meaning when these polytheists are terrified at the time of death—**فَلَا فَوْتَ** — "and there is no escape"—meaning they do not escape Allah by fleeing, fortifying themselves, or any other barrier.

His saying: **وَأُخِذُوا مِنْ مَكَانٍ قَرِيبٍ** — "and they are seized from a place nearby" is a metonymy for the lack of separation between them and the one who seizes them. He expressed it with **أُخِذُوا** — "they are seized" in the passive voice so that the seizing is attributed to Him, the Glorified. He has described Himself as being near, and He revealed the meaning of His nearness in His saying: **وَنَحْنُ أَقْرَبُ إِلَيْهِ مِنْكُمْ وَلَكِنْ لَا تُبْصِرُونَ** — "and We are nearer to him than you are, though you do not perceive" (56:85), and even more so in His saying: **مِنْ حَبْلِ الْوَرِيدِ** — "[nearer to him] than his jugular vein" (40:16), and even more so in His saying: **أَنَّ اللَّهَ يَحُولُ بَيْنَ الْمَرْءِ وَقَلْبِهِ** — "that Allah intervenes between a man and his heart" (8:24). Thus, He clarified that He is nearer to man than his own self. This position is the "watchtower" (المرصاد) which He mentioned in His saying: **إِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَبِالْمِرْصَادِ** — "Indeed your Lord is in ambush" (89:14). How then can a person's escape from Him be conceived when He is nearer to him than his own self? Or from His noble angels who receive the command from Him, the Exalted, without any barrier veiling them from Him or any intermediary between Him and them.

Thus, His saying: **وَأُخِذُوا مِنْ مَكَانٍ قَرِيبٍ** — "and they are seized from a place nearby" is a type of representation (تمثيل) of His nearness to man according to what we conceive of the meaning of nearness due to our confinement in the prison of time and space and our familiarity with material things; otherwise, the matter is greater than that.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **وَقَالُوا آمَنَّا بِهِ وَأَنَّى لَهُمُ التَّنَاوُشُ مِنْ مَكَانٍ بَعِيدٍ** — "They will say, ‘We believe in it.’ But how can they attain [faith] from a place far away?" (34:52). *Tanawush* (التناوش) is attaining or reaching. The pronoun in "in it" (به) refers to the Quran, as provided by the context.

What is meant by their being in a "place far away" is that they are in the world of the Hereafter, which is the abode of the determination of recompense, and it is the furthest thing from the world of the Dunya, which is the abode of action and the place of acquisition by choice. The Unseen (الغيب) — *that which lies beyond sensory perception and empirical knowledge* — has turned into witness for them, and the witness into Unseen, as the following verse indicates.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **وَقَدْ كَفَرُوا بِهِ مِنْ قَبْلُ وَيَقْذِفُونَ بِالْغَيْبِ مِنْ مَكَانٍ بَعِيدٍ** — "They had certainly disbelieved in it before, and they would conjecture about the Unseen from a place far away" (34:53). This is a circumstantial clause for the pronoun in "But how can they attain." What is meant by His saying: **وَيَقْذِفُونَ بِالْغَيْبِ مِنْ مَكَانٍ بَعِيدٍ** — "and they would conjecture about the Unseen from a place far away" is their hurling conjectures at the world of the Hereafter while they were in the Dunya, despite their lack of knowledge of it and its being hidden from their senses, as they used to say: there is no resurrection, no paradise, and no fire. It is also said: it refers to their hurling accusations of magic, lying, fabrication, and poetry at the Prophet صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله.

The point in applying "place far away" to the Dunya in relation to the Hereafter is similar to its application to the Hereafter in relation to the Dunya, as previously indicated.

The meaning of the two verses is: the polytheists said when they were seized, "We believe in the truth which is the Quran," but how can they attain faith in it—a faith that benefits with salvation—from a place far away, which is the Hereafter, while the case is that they disbelieved in it before in the Dunya, and they used to negate the matters of the Hereafter with conjectures and illusions from a place far away, which is the Dunya.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **وَحِيلَ بَيْنَهُمْ وَبَيْنَ مَا يَشْتَهُونَ كَمَا فُعِلَ بِأَشْيَاعِهِمْ مِنْ قَبْلُ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا فِي شَكٍّ مُرِيبٍ** — "A barrier will be set between them and what they desire, as was done with their likes before. Indeed they were in a disquieting doubt" (34:54). The apparent context is that what is meant by "what they desire" are the material worldly pleasures which are barred from them by death. What is meant by "their likes before" (أشياعهم من قبل) are those similar to them from the past nations or those who agreed with them in creed. His saying: **إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا فِي شَكٍّ مُرِيبٍ** — "Indeed they were in a disquieting doubt" is the reason for His saying: "as was done..." etc.

The meaning is: a barrier was placed between the seized polytheists and what they desire of worldly pleasures, just as was done with their likes among the polytheists of the passing nations before them; indeed, they were in a disquieting doubt regarding the truth or the Hereafter, so they would conjecture about it from the Unseen.

Know that what we have presented of the discourse on these four verses is based on what the apparent context provides. However, narrations have been widely transmitted (استفاضت) through the paths of the Shi'a and the Sunnis that the verses refer to the swallowing up of the army of the Sufyani in the desert (البيداء), which is one of the signs of the appearance of the Mahdi (peace be upon him) connected to it. On the premise that the verses were revealed regarding that, what we have presented of the meaning would be by way of the verses' application (جري) to it.

## (Narrative Discussion)

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi, regarding His saying, the Exalted: **وَأَسَرُّوا النَّدَامَةَ لَمَّا رَأَوُا الْعَذَابَ** — "And they will hide their remorse when they see the punishment," he said: "They hide their remorse in the Fire when they see the Friend of Allah (ولي الله)." It was asked: "O son of the Messenger of Allah, what does their hiding of remorse avail them while they are in the punishment?" He replied: "They dislike the gloating of the enemies."

**I say**: It was also narrated from Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him).

In the same source, a man mentioned the wealthy in the presence of Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him) and spoke ill of them. Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him) said: "Be silent! For if the wealthy person maintains his ties of kinship and is kind to his brothers, Allah doubles the reward for him twofold, because Allah says: **وَمَا أَمْوَالُكُمْ وَلَا أَوْلَادُكُمْ بِالَّتِي تُقَرِّبُكُمْ عِنْدَنَا زُلْفَى إِلَّا مَنْ آمَنَ وَعَمِلَ صَالِحًا فَأُولَئِكَ لَهُمْ جَزَاءُ الضِّعْفِ بِمَا عَمِلُوا وَهُمْ فِي الْغُرُفَاتِ آمِنُونَ** — 'It is not your wealth or your children that will bring you near to Us in station, except those who have faith and act righteously. For such there will be a twofold reward for what they did, and they will be secure in the high chambers' (34:37)."

In the *Amali* of the Shaykh, with his chain to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a hadith in which he says: "**حتى إذا كان يوم القيامة حسب لهم ـ ثم أعطاهم بكل واحدة عشر أمثالها إلى سبعمائة ضعف قال الله عز وجل : « جَزاءً مِنْ رَبِّكَ عَطاءً حِساباً » وقال : « فَأُولئِكَ لَهُمْ جَزاءُ الضِّعْفِ بِما عَمِلُوا ـ وَهُمْ فِي الْغُرُفاتِ آمِنُونَ »**" — "Until when it is the Day of Resurrection, it is reckoned for them, then He gives them for every one ten times its like up to seven hundred fold. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: 'A reward from your Lord, a calculated gift' (78:36), and He said: 'For such there will be a twofold reward for what they did, and they will be secure in the high chambers' (34:37)."

In *al-Kafi*, with his chain from al-Sakuni from Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله said: "**من صدق بالخلف جاد بالعطية**" — "Whoever believes in the replacement is generous in giving."

In the same source, with his chain from Sama'ah from Abu al-Hasan (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله said: "**من أيقن بالخلف سخت نفسه بالنفقة**" — "Whoever is certain of the replacement, his soul becomes generous in spending."

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, Ibn Mardawayh extracted from Ali ibn Abi Talib: I heard the Messenger of Allah صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله saying: "**إن لكل يوم نحسا فادفعوا نحس ذلك اليوم بالصدقة**" — "Indeed, for every day there is an ill-omen, so repel the ill-omen of that day with charity." Then he said: "Read the places of replacement, for I heard Allah saying: **وَمَا أَنْفَقْتُمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ فَهُوَ يُخْلِفُهُ** — 'and He will replace whatever thing you spend' (34:39). If they do not spend, how shall He replace?"

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi, in the narration of Abu al-Jarud from Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him) regarding His saying, the Exalted: **قُلْ مَا سَأَلْتُكُمْ مِنْ أَجْرٍ فَهُوَ لَكُمْ** — "Say, ‘Whatever reward I may have asked of you, it is for yourselves’" (34:47): "That was because the Messenger of Allah صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله asked his people to love his relatives and not to harm them. As for His saying: **فَهُوَ لَكُمْ** — 'it is for yourselves,' He says: its reward is for you."

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, regarding His saying, the Exalted: **وَلَوْ تَرَى إِذْ فَزِعُوا** — "Were you to see when they are struck with terror" (34:51), al-Hakim extracted and authenticated from Abu Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of Allah صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله said: "**يخرج رجل يقال له السفياني في عمق دمشق ـ وعامة من يتبعه من كلب فيقتل حتى يبقر بطون النساء ـ ويقتل الصبيان فيجمع لهم قيس فيقتلها حتى لا يمنع ذنب تلعة ـ ويخرج رجل من أهل بيتي فيبلغ السفياني فيبعث إليه جندا من جنده ـ فيهزمهم فيسير إليه السفياني بمن معه ـ حتى إذا صار ببيداء من الأرض خسف بهم ـ فلا ينجو منهم إلا المخبر منهم**" — "A man called the Sufyani will emerge from the depths of Damascus, and most of those who follow him will be from Kalb. He will kill until he rips open the bellies of women and kills children. Qays will gather against them, and he will kill them until not even the tail of a hillock is defended. Then a man from my household will emerge, and news will reach the Sufyani, so he will send an army from his troops against him, but he will defeat them. Then the Sufyani will march toward him with those with him, until when he is in a desert (بيداء) of the earth, they are swallowed up, and none of them shall escape except the one who reports it."

**I say**: The narration is widely transmitted (مستفيضة) through the paths of the Sunnis, in brief or in detail. They have narrated it through various paths from Ibn Abbas, Ibn Mas'ud, Hudhayfah, Abu Hurayrah, the grandfather of Amr ibn Shu'ayb, Umm Salamah, Safiyyah, Aisha, and Hafsah—the wives of the Prophet صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله—and Nufayrah the wife of al-Qa'qa' from Sa'id ibn Jubayr as a suspended (موقوف) report.

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi, regarding His saying, the Exalted: **وَلَوْ تَرَى إِذْ فَزِعُوا فَلَا فَوْتَ** — "Were you to see when they are struck with terror, and there is no escape" (34:51): My father narrated to me from Ibn Abi Umayr from Mansur ibn Yunus from Abu Khalid al-Kabuli, who said: Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him) said: "By Allah, it is as if I am looking at the Riser (القائم) (peace be upon him) having leaned his back against the Stone, then he adjures Allah for his right, then he says: 'O people, whoever disputes with me concerning Allah, I am the foremost with Allah. O people, whoever disputes with me concerning Adam, I am the foremost with Adam. O people, whoever disputes with me concerning Noah, I am the foremost with Noah. O people, whoever disputes with me concerning Abraham, I am the foremost with Abraham. O people, whoever disputes with me concerning Moses, I am the foremost with Moses. O people, whoever disputes with me concerning Jesus, I am the foremost with Jesus. O people, whoever disputes with me concerning Muhammad, I am the foremost with Muhammad. O people, whoever disputes with me concerning the Book of Allah, I am the foremost with the Book of Allah.'

Then he reaches the Station (المقام) and prays two units of prayer and adjures Allah for his right." Then Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him) said: "He, by Allah, is the 'distressed one' (المضطر) in the Book of Allah, in His saying: **أَمَّنْ يُجِيبُ الْمُضْطَرَّ إِذَا دَعَاهُ وَيَكْشِفُ السُّوءَ وَيَجْعَلُكُمْ خُلَفَاءَ الْأَرْضِ** — 'Is not He who answers the distressed one when he calls Him, and removes his distress, and makes you successors on the earth?' (27:62).

The first to pledge allegiance to him will be Gabriel, then the three hundred and thirteen. Whoever was tested with the journey will arrive, and whoever was not tested with the journey will have disappeared from his bed. That is the saying of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): 'They are the ones missing from their beds,' and that is the saying of Allah: **فَاسْتَبِقُوا الْخَيْرَاتِ أَيْنَ مَا تَكُونُوا يَأْتِ بِكُمُ اللَّهُ جَمِيعًا** — 'so take the lead in all good works. Wherever you may be, Allah will bring you all together' (2:148). He said: 'The good works (الخيرات) is the guardianship (الولاية).' And He said in another place: **وَلَئِنْ أَخَّرْنَا عَنْهُمُ الْعَذَابَ إِلَى أُمَّةٍ مَعْدُودَةٍ** — 'And if We defer their punishment until a reckoned community' (11:8)—and they are the companions of the Riser (peace be upon him), they will gather, by Allah, to him in a single hour.

When he comes to the desert (البيداء), the army of the Sufyani will come out against him, so Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will command the earth and it will seize their feet. That is His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: **وَلَوْ تَرَى إِذْ فَزِعُوا فَلَا فَوْتَ وَأُخِذُوا مِنْ مَكَانٍ قَرِيبٍ وَقَالُوا آمَنَّا بِهِ** — 'Were you to see when they are struck with terror, and there is no escape, and they are seized from a place nearby and they say, "We believe in it"'—meaning in the Riser from the family of Muhammad (peace be upon him)—**وَأَنَّى لَهُمُ التَّنَاوُشُ مِنْ مَكَانٍ بَعِيدٍ وَحِيلَ بَيْنَهُمْ وَبَيْنَ مَا يَشْتَهُونَ** — 'But how can they attain [faith] from a place far away? A barrier will be set between them and what they desire'—meaning that they not be punished—**كَمَا فُعِلَ بِأَشْيَاعِهِمْ** — 'as was done with their likes'—meaning those before them of the deniers who perished—**مِنْ قَبْلُ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا فِي شَكٍّ مُرِيبٍ** — 'before. Indeed they were in a disquieting doubt.'"

— Completed, and praise be to Allah —
